summary: Fixed bug where cost fields (Valor Nova/Valor Reforma) were not being saved to Supabase. Root cause: saveTool function was trying to save 'is_reformed' and 'reform_count' columns that don't exist in the Supabase tools table. Removed these columns from the save operation.
passed_tests: Cost fields (Valor Nova, Valor Reforma) can be filled with decimal values
Save button works without errors after fix
Values appear in the tools list after save (PriceTag component)
Values persist after page reload (Supabase persistence verified)
Values appear in the details modal (Sheet component)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cost fields (unit_value, reform_unit_value) are now saving correctly to Supabase. The fix removed 'is_reformed' and 'reform_count' columns from the saveTool function as they don't exist in the Supabase tools table schema.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The saveTool function in /app/frontend/lib/data-store.tsx was including 'is_reformed' and 'reform_count' columns in the database object, but these columns don't exist in the Supabase 'tools' table. This caused a PGRST204 error: 'Could not find the is_reformed column of tools in the schema cache'
reproduction_steps: Click on a tool's action menu and select 'Editar'
Fill in 'Valor Nova (R$)' and 'Valor Reforma (R$)' fields
Console shows error: PGRST204 - Could not find the 'is_reformed' column
Values don't persist after page reload
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Removed 'is_reformed: tool.isReformed || false' and 'reform_count: tool.reformCount || 0' from the dbTool object in the saveTool function
